content_en:
‘Abdullah ibn Saabit said, “When death approached Abu Bakr #, he asked that ‘Umar # should come, and he # said, ‘Indeed, only his balances are heavy whose balances are heavy on_the Pay of Resurrection for following the truth in the world and for that béing difficult upon them (i.e., in terms of hardships). It is appropriate for the balance to be heavy when the truth is placed in it, And only those balances are light — of those whose balances Bre light — for following falsehood in the world and for that being easy upon them (ie. for them having taken the easy path of sin and of following desires). And it is appropriate for a balance to be light tomorrow when falsehood is placed in it.”

A Good Character Is The Heaviest Thing That Will Be Placed In The Balance Of The Slave On The Day Of Resurrection .

Ahaadeeth have been related about the weighing of deeds themselves, as has been related in Safech Muslim, from Abu

Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations 385

And the following is established in another hadith: “Al-Bagarah and Aai-Traan (the Chapters of the Qur’an) will come on the Day of Resurrection as if they are two clouds or two Ghayaabahs (anything that gives shade, like a cloud or anything else) or birds, arguing on behalf of its Saahib (literally, holder; meaning, those who would

386 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

recite them, etc.).” (Ahmad) This means that, on the Day of Resurrection, the reward of reciting them turns into what is mentioned.

And he 28 said, “Recite if you wish: ior area ee yy BM me 4S OT ip A ch Sep

“And on the Day on Resurrection, We shall assign no weight for

them’) A similar narration is related through a different chain from Abu Hurairah :. In it, the Messenger of Allah #8 said, “A gluttonous, heavy drinking, large man will be brought, and he will be weighed along (i.e. in comparison to) with a grain, and he will be less than it in weight.” (Related by Ibn Abee Haatim) ‘Abdullah ibn Buraidah related that his father # said, “We were with the Messenger of Allah # when a man from the Quraish came, strutting in some kind of robe. When he stood over the Prophet 3%, the Prophet 2 said, ‘O Abu Buraidah, this one is from those about whom Allah said: And on the Day of Resurrection, We shall assign no weight for them.’” (Related by Al-Bazzaar)

Zurr ibn Habeesh related from Ibn Mas‘ood # that he 4 had very thin legs. (One day) the wind flung away (his garment, thus exposing them). The people laughed at him. Then the Messenger of Allah g said, “What are you laughing at?” They said, “O Prophet of Allah, from the thinness of his legs.” He &¥ said, “By the One Who has my soul in His Hand, the two of them (i.e. his two legs) are indeed heavier in the Balance than Uhud (a large mountain in AlMadeenah).” (Almnad) Ahmad alone related it, and its chain is good

Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations 387

There are other narrations that mention similar descriptions... And Allah 3% knows best.

‘Aishah % said, “O Messenger of Allah, will you remember your family on the Day of Resurrection?” He #4 said, “As for three places, then no: the book, the Balance, and the Siraat (which is over the Hellfire, and which people will cross over — those crossing over being the saved ones).”

In this hadith, “the book” might be referring to when the book of deeds is placed, to bear witness over the Nations with their deeds; or it might be referring to the scrolls that will scatter, and people will differ in that; some will be taking them by their right, while others are taking them by their left.

In another narration, ‘Aishah 4% mentioned the Hellfire and cried. The wording is the same as the hadith above except that the Prophet 2% said, “And at the Book, when it will be said, ‘Come and read my book,’ until he knows where his book will fall - in his right, in his left, or from behind his back; at the Siraat, when he is placed over the Hellfire.” (Al-Baihagee)

Another Chain From ‘Aishah Bint Abu Bakr &

‘Aishah %& said, “I said to the Messenger of Allah #%, ‘Does the lover yemember the one he loves on the Day of Resurrection?’ He #8 said, ‘O‘Aishah, as for three (situations), then no: at the Balance until it is heavy or light, then no; when the books are scattered, and one is given his either with his right or with his left, then no; and when a neck from the Hellfire comes out and embraces them, raging over them, it will say, ‘I have been entrusted with three ~ with he who

388 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

claims a god besides Allah; I have been entrusted with he who does not believe in the Day of Al-Hisaab (accountability); and I have been entrusted with the haughty, stubborn man.’ It will embrace them (those three categories of people) and throw them in the midst of the Hellfire. And the Hellfire has a bridge (over it) that is thinner than hair and sharper than the sword; upon it are hooks and Hasak (sharp thorns made from iron) — taking whomsoever Allah pleases. People will go (cross) over it like the blinking of the eye, like lightning, like racing horses and mounts. And the angels will be saying, ‘Our Lord, Sallim (save, deliver, protect), Our Lord, Sallim. There will be he who is saved and is in good condition, he who is saved but is scratched, and he who is rolled over into the Hellfire on his face.” (Ahmad)

Anas ibn Maalik # related that the Prophet 2% said, “The son of Adam will be brought on the Day of Resurrection, and he will be made to stand between the pans (scales) of the Balance. An angel will be appointed to him, so that if his scale is heavy, the angel will call out with a voice that will reach all created beings: ‘So-and-so has achieved a happiness after which he will never again be wretched.’ But if his scale is light, then the angel will call out with a voice that all created beings will hear, ‘So-and-so has become wretched with a wretchedness after which he will never again be happy.” Abu Bakr Al-Baihagee related this narration and then said, “Its chain is weak.”
content_id:
'Abdullah ibn Saabit berkata, "Ketika kematian mendekati Abu Bakr #, beliau meminta agar 'Umar # datang, dan beliau # berkata, 'Sesungguhnya hanya orang-orang yang berat timbangannya — timbangan mereka berat pada Hari Kebangkitan karena mengikuti kebenaran di dunia dan karena hal itu berat bagi mereka (yakni dalam hal kesulitan). Sudah selayaknya timbangan menjadi berat ketika kebenaran diletakkan di atasnya. Dan hanya orang-orang yang ringan timbangannya — timbangan mereka ringan — karena mengikuti kebatilan di dunia dan karena hal itu mudah bagi mereka (yakni karena mereka telah mengambil jalan mudah berupa dosa dan mengikuti hawa nafsu). Dan sudah selayaknya timbangan menjadi ringan esok hari ketika kebatilan diletakkan di atasnya.'

Akhlak yang Baik Adalah Hal Terberat yang Akan Diletakkan dalam Timbangan Hamba pada Hari Kebangkitan.

Hadith-hadith telah diriwayatkan tentang penimbangan amal-amal itu sendiri, sebagaimana diriwayatkan dalam Sahih Muslim, dari Abu

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 385

Dan yang berikut ini ditetapkan dalam hadith lain: "Al-Baqarah dan Ali Imran (surah-surah dalam Al-Qur'an) akan datang pada Hari Kebangkitan seolah-olah keduanya adalah dua awan atau dua Ghayaabaah (apa pun yang memberikan naungan, seperti awan atau yang lainnya) atau burung-burung, berdebat membela Sahibnya (secara harfiah, pemilik; maksudnya, mereka yang

386 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

membacanya, dll.)." (Ahmad) Ini berarti bahwa, pada Hari Kebangkitan, pahala membacanya berubah menjadi apa yang disebutkan.

Dan beliau # bersabda, "Bacalah jika kamu mau: [ayat Arab]

"Dan pada Hari Kebangkitan, Kami tidak akan memberikan bobot apa pun untuk mereka."

Riwayat serupa diriwayatkan melalui rantai yang berbeda dari Abu Hurairah #. Di dalamnya, Rasulullah # bersabda, "Seorang pria yang rakus, banyak minum, dan berbadan besar akan dibawa, dan dia akan ditimbang bersamaan (yakni dibandingkan) dengan sebutir biji, dan dia akan lebih ringan darinya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Abee Haatim) 'Abdullah ibn Buraidah meriwayatkan bahwa ayahnya # berkata, "Kami sedang bersama Rasulullah # ketika seorang pria dari Quraisy datang, berjalan dengan congkak dalam sejenis jubah. Ketika dia berdiri di depan Nabi #, Nabi # bersabda, 'Wahai Abu Buraidah, orang ini termasuk dari mereka yang Allah berfirman tentangnya: Dan pada Hari Kebangkitan, Kami tidak akan memberikan bobot apa pun untuk mereka.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bazzaar)

Zurr ibn Habeesh meriwayatkan dari Ibn Mas'ood # bahwa beliau # memiliki kaki yang sangat kurus. (Suatu hari) angin menerbangkan (pakaiannya, sehingga memperlihatkan kaki-kakinya). Orang-orang menertawakannya. Kemudian Rasulullah # bersabda, "Apa yang membuat kalian tertawa?" Mereka berkata, "Wahai Nabi Allah, karena kekurusan kaki-kakinya." Beliau # bersabda, "Demi Dzat yang jiwaku ada di tangan-Nya, keduanya (yakni kedua kakinya) sungguh lebih berat dalam Timbangan daripada Uhud (gunung besar di Al-Madinah)." (Ahmad) Hanya Ahmad yang meriwayatkannya, dan sanadnya baik.

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 387

Ada riwayat-riwayat lain yang menyebutkan gambaran serupa... Dan Allah # Mahatahu.

'Aisyah # berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, apakah engkau akan mengingat keluargamu pada Hari Kebangkitan?" Beliau # bersabda, "Adapun tiga tempat, maka tidak: kitab, Timbangan, dan Sirat (yang berada di atas Neraka, dan yang akan diseberangi orang-orang — mereka yang menyeberanginya adalah orang-orang yang selamat)."

Dalam hadith ini, "kitab" mungkin merujuk kepada ketika buku amal diletakkan, untuk menjadi saksi atas umat-umat dengan perbuatan mereka; atau mungkin merujuk kepada lembaran-lembaran yang akan berterbangan, dan orang-orang akan berbeda dalam hal itu; sebagian akan mengambilnya dengan tangan kanan mereka, sementara yang lain mengambilnya dengan tangan kiri mereka.

Dalam riwayat lain, 'Aisyah # menyebut Neraka dan menangis. Redaksinya sama dengan hadith di atas kecuali bahwa Nabi # bersabda, "Dan pada Kitab, ketika akan dikatakan, 'Datanglah dan bacalah kitabmu,' sampai dia mengetahui di mana kitabnya akan jatuh — di tangan kanannya, di tangan kirinya, atau dari belakang punggungnya; di Sirat, ketika dia diletakkan di atas Neraka." (Al-Baihaqee)

Rantai Lain dari 'Aisyah Binti Abu Bakr #

'Aisyah # berkata, "Aku berkata kepada Rasulullah #, 'Apakah orang yang mencintai mengingat orang yang dicintainya pada Hari Kebangkitan?' Beliau # bersabda, 'Wahai 'Aisyah, adapun tiga (situasi), maka tidak: di Timbangan sampai ia berat atau ringan, maka tidak; ketika lembaran-lembaran berterbangan, dan seseorang diberikan (kitabnya) dengan tangan kanan atau tangan kirinya, maka tidak; dan ketika leher dari Neraka keluar dan merangkul mereka, mengamuk terhadap mereka, ia akan berkata, "Aku telah diberi amanat tiga — dia yang mengklaim tuhan selain Allah; aku telah diberi amanat dia yang tidak beriman pada Hari Al-Hisab (pertanggungjawaban); dan aku telah diberi amanat pria yang sombong dan keras kepala." Ia akan merangkul mereka (tiga golongan orang tersebut) dan melemparkan mereka ke tengah-tengah Neraka. Dan Neraka memiliki jembatan (di atasnya) yang lebih tipis dari rambut dan lebih tajam dari pedang; di atasnya terdapat kait-kait dan Hasak (duri tajam yang terbuat dari besi) — menangkap siapa pun yang Allah kehendaki. Orang-orang akan melewatinya (menyeberangi) seperti kedipan mata, seperti kilat, seperti kuda dan tunggangan yang berlomba. Dan para malaikat akan berkata, "Ya Tuhan kami, Sallim (selamatkan, lindungi), Ya Tuhan kami, Sallim." Akan ada orang yang selamat dan dalam kondisi baik, orang yang selamat tetapi terluka, dan orang yang terguling ke dalam Neraka dengan mukanya." (Ahmad)

Anas ibn Maalik # meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi # bersabda, "Anak cucu Adam akan dibawa pada Hari Kebangkitan, dan dia akan dibuat berdiri di antara dua sisi (timbangan) Neraca. Seorang malaikat akan ditetapkan untuknya, sehingga jika timbangannya berat, malaikat itu akan berseru dengan suara yang akan terdengar oleh semua makhluk: 'Si fulan telah meraih kebahagiaan yang setelahnya dia tidak akan pernah lagi sengsara.' Namun jika timbangannya ringan, maka malaikat itu akan berseru dengan suara yang akan didengar semua makhluk, 'Si fulan telah sengsara dengan kesengsaraan yang setelahnya dia tidak akan pernah lagi bahagia.'" Abu Bakr Al-Baihaqee meriwayatkan narasi ini dan kemudian berkata, "Sanadnya lemah."
content_ar: ‘Abdullah ibn Saabit said, “عندما الموت approached أبو بكر #, he asked that ‘عمر # should come, and he # said, ‘Indeed, only his balances are heavy whose balances are heavy on_the Pay of البعث for following the الحق in the الدنيا and for that béing difficult upon them (i.e., in terms of hardships). It is appropriate for the balance to be heavy عندما the الحق is placed in it, And only those balances are light — of those whose balances Bre light — for following الباطل in the الدنيا and for that being easy upon them (ie. for them having taken the easy الطريق of الذنب and of following desires). And it is appropriate for a balance to be light tomorrow عندما الباطل is placed in it.” A الخير الأخلاق Is The Heaviest Thing That Will Be Placed In The Balance Of The Slave On The Day Of البعث . Ahaadeeth have been related about the weighing of deeds themselves, as has been related in Safech مسلم, from Abu الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 385 And the following is established in another الحديث: “Al-Bagarah and Aai-Traan (the الأبواب of the Qur’an) will come on the Day of البعث as إذا they are two clouds or two Ghayaabahs (anything that gives shade, like a cloud or anything else) or birds, arguing on behalf of its Saahib (literally, holder; meaning, those who would 386 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations recite them, etc.).” (أحمد) This means that, on the Day of البعث, the الأجر of reciting them turns into what is mentioned. And he 28 said, “Recite إذا you wish: ior area ee yy BM me 4S OT ip A ch Sep “And on the Day on البعث, We shall assign no weight for them’) A similar narration is related through a different chain from أبو هريرة :. In it, الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said, “A gluttonous, heavy drinking, large man will be brought, and he will be weighed along (i.e. in comparison to) with a grain, and he will be less than it in weight.” (Related by Ibn Abee Haatim) ‘Abdullah ibn Buraidah related that his الأب # said, “We were with الرسول ﷺ of الله # عندما a man from the Quraish came, strutting in some kind of robe. عندما he stood over النبي ﷺ 3%, النبي ﷺ 2 said, ‘O Abu Buraidah, this one is from those about whom الله said: And on the Day of البعث, We shall assign no weight for them.’” (Related by Al-Bazzaar) Zurr ibn Habeesh related from Ibn Mas‘ood # that he 4 had very thin legs. (يوماً ما) the wind flung away (his garment, وهكذا exposing them). The people laughed at him. Then الرسول ﷺ of الله g said, “What are you laughing at?” قالوا, “O النبي of الله, from the thinness of his legs.” He &¥ said, “By the One Who has my النفس in His Hand, the two of them (i.e. his two legs) are indeed heavier in the Balance than Uhud (a large mountain in AlMadeenah).” (Almnad) أحمد alone related it, and its chain is الخير الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 387 There are other narrations that mention similar descriptions... And الله 3% knows best. ‘عائشة % said, “O Messenger of الله, will you remember your الأسرة on the Day of البعث?” He #4 said, “أما three places, then no: the الكتاب, the Balance, and the Siraat (which is over the النار, and which people will cross over — those crossing over being the saved ones).” In this الحديث, “the الكتاب” might be referring to عندما the الكتاب of deeds is placed, to bear witness over the Nations with their deeds; or it might be referring to the scrolls that will scatter, and people will differ in that; some will be taking them by their الحق, بينما others are taking them by their left. In another narration, ‘عائشة 4% mentioned the النار and cried. The wording is the same as the الحديث above except that النبي ﷺ 2% said, “And at the الكتاب, عندما it will be said, ‘Come and read my الكتاب,’ حتى he knows where his الكتاب will fall - in his الحق, in his left, or from behind his back; at the Siraat, عندما he is placed over the النار.” (Al-Baihagee) Another Chain From ‘عائشة Bint أبو بكر & ‘عائشة %& said, “I said to الرسول ﷺ of الله #%, ‘Does the lover yemember the one he loves on the Day of البعث?’ He #8 said, ‘O‘عائشة, أما three (situations), then no: at the Balance حتى it is heavy or light, then no; عندما the الكتب are scattered, and one is given his either with his الحق or with his left, then no; and عندما a neck from the النار comes out and embraces them, raging over them, it will say, ‘I have been entrusted with three ~ with he who 388 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations claims a god بالإضافة إلى الله; I have been entrusted with he who does not believe in the Day of Al-Hisaab (accountability); and I have been entrusted with the haughty, stubborn man.’ It will embrace them (those three categories of people) and throw them in the midst of the النار. And the النار has a bridge (over it) that is thinner than hair and sharper than the sword; upon it are hooks and Hasak (sharp thorns made from iron) — taking whomsoever الله pleases. People will go (cross) over it like the blinking of the eye, like lightning, like racing horses and mounts. And the الملائكة will be saying, ‘Our Lord, Sallim (save, deliver, protect), Our Lord, Sallim. There will be he who is saved and is in الخير condition, he who is saved but is scratched, and he who is rolled over into the النار on his face.” (أحمد) أنس ibn Maalik # related that النبي ﷺ 2% said, “The son of آدم will be brought on the Day of البعث, and he will be made to stand between the pans (scales) of the Balance. An angel will be appointed to him, لذا that إذا his scale is heavy, the angel will call out with a voice that will reach all created beings: ‘لذا-and-لذا has achieved a happiness بعد which he will never again be wretched.’ But إذا his scale is light, then the angel will call out with a voice that all created beings will hear, ‘لذا-and-لذا has become wretched with a wretchedness بعد which he will never again be happy.” أبو بكر Al-Baihagee related this narration and then said, “Its chain is weak.”
